text.title: Two-Pass: Free-Form Reasoning Then Structured Extraction Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the two-pass-reason-then-extract methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Tasks combining deep reasoning + strict schema MUST split into two passes: strong model in free text (or extended thinking), then cheap model with strict structured output over the strong-model transcript.
rule.rationale: SLOT (arXiv:2505.04016) + BuildMVPFast 2026 report 10-15% accuracy drop on math/analysis under strict SO vs free text. Two-pass restores it at near-zero extraction cost.

rule.statement: Pass 1 MUST NOT use `tool_choice` / `response_format=json_schema` / strict grammar constraints. Extended thinking is allowed and encouraged when task difficulty justifies it.
rule.rationale: The grammar mask costs probability mass on tokens that are not the optimal reasoning tokens; removing it is what recovers accuracy.

rule.statement: Pass 2 MUST use strict structured output (`response_format=json_schema` / `tool_choice` / Pydantic `parse`). Temperature ≤ 0.2.

rationale.source: Anthropic tool-use docs; OpenAI structured outputs docs

rule.rationale: Extraction is mechanical; determinism is the contract the consumer relies on.

rule.statement: The strong-model transcript passed to the extractor MUST be capped (e.g. 8K tokens). If reasoning exceeds the cap, summarize first or split the task.
rule.rationale: Long transcripts inflate extractor cost and erode the savings; bounded transcripts keep the 2-pass cost ratio favourable.

rule.statement: Before deploying two-pass, run an A/B vs single-pass on ≥50 representative tasks. Adopt only when accuracy delta ≥ 5% absolute and cost delta ≤ 2x.
rule.rationale: Two-pass is an investment; A/B keeps the decision data-driven and prevents cargo-culting on tasks where single-pass is fine.
